events.text: Jennifer Perez, 57-year-old White female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Adenocarcinoma of the Right upper lobe of lung. Clinical staging: T2aN2M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C34.11. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 rash (acneiform). Treated with doxycycline 100 mg BID and topical clindamycin gel.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Underwent surgical resection: Right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 4 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Completed definitive chemoradiation: Concurrent chemoradiation 60 Gy in 30 fractions with weekly carboplatin/paclitaxel. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Perez
MRN: patient_2812c8f3ff93
Date of Study: 2022-07-19

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 4.4 x 3.5 cm. New finding.

MEDIASTINUM: Right hilar lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.9 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Perez
MRN: patient_2812c8f3ff93
Specimen ID: S2022-27092
Date of Procedure: 2022-07-25
Date of Report: 2022-07-29

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.9 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jennifer Perez - Right upper lobe of lung" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 1.9 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jennifer Perez
MRN: patient_2812c8f3ff93
Date: 2022-07-30
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Perez is a 57-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- type 2 diabetes mellitus
- hyperlipidemia

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: Codeine (nausea)

MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: constipation, dry mouth, night sweats. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 121/68, HR 66, RR 14, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 85.5 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.8, Hgb 10.0, Plt 110, Cr 0.97

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
57-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jennifer Perez
MRN: patient_2812c8f3ff93
Date of Report: 2022-08-10
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.8 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R248W - Missense (Exon 7)
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 659x coverage.
Tumor content: 41%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

Adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.

Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jennifer Perez - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 6.1 x 4.3 x 3.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Patient: Jennifer Perez

Date of Study: 2023-12-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 2.4 x 1.9 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Right hilar lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.7 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

scenario_blurb: Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation
